pages.raw_text:
w. B. Reinwald, city milk inspec-
tor, sees in the summer fallow
lands in the Palouse the future
hope of an abundant milk supply
for Spokane.


For years the farmers of the
Palouse have permitted their lands
to lie fallow every other year with
out producing anything.


Through the activity of Mr.. Rein
wald three large ranchers were
induced to put in silos and plant
corn for fodder in the land previ-
ously intended for fallow. This
corn was placed in silage and has
proved a great success in feeding
dairy cows. The farmers that
have tried it have made money by
sending the milk to Spokane. and
others will probably now follow
the lead.


Cow feed of this character costs
only a fraction of what alfalfa
does.
